title: Roasted Algerian Chicken Tacos
description: A flavorful and crunchy one-pot dish featuring roasted chicken, hard taco shells, and Algerian spices, all made in a paella pan. This recipe is onion and garlic free, making it friendly for those with sensitivities.
instructions: Preheat the oven to 425 degrees Fahrenheit.
In a large paella pan, heat the olive oil over medium high heat.
Add the chicken to the pan and cook until browned on all sides, about 5 minutes.
Add the cumin, paprika, salt, and black pepper to the pan and stir to combine.
Transfer the pan to the preheated oven and roast the chicken for 20 minutes.
While the chicken is cooking, prepare the Algerian spice blend according to the package instructions.
After the chicken has roasted for 20 minutes, remove the pan from the oven and stir in the Algerian spice blend and lemon juice.
Add the hard taco shells to the pan and stir to combine with the chicken and spices.
Return the pan to the oven and cook for an additional 5 minutes, or until the taco shells are crispy.
Remove the pan from the oven and let cool for a few minutes before serving.
